questions.question: What happens in the body during a cardiac arrest?
options: Blood pressure rises sharply but circulation continues
The lungs slowly fill with fluid over several hours
The brain swells and pushes against the skull
Blood stops flowing to the brain and vital organs

questions.question: Which guidance is this BLS awareness course based on?
options: Resuscitation Council UK guidelines and NHS guidance

questions.question: How often will your employer usually arrange BLS refresher sessions?
options: Only when a new member of staff joins

questions.question: According to the course, how quickly can resuscitation skills fade after training?
options: Not for at least ten years

questions.question: In the DRABC approach, what does the 'R' stand for?
How should you open the airway of an unresponsive person?
options: Press firmly on the top of the head
Turn their head fully to one side
Lift both shoulders off the ground

questions.question: Why can an unresponsive person's airway become blocked?
For how long should you look, listen and feel for normal breathing?
When checking for normal breathing, what should you feel for?
If someone has a seizure, what does the course advise you to do?
options: Hold their limbs firmly still until it stops
Protect them from injury and check them once it passes

questions.question: What should you do with your arms while giving chest compressions?
options: Keep your shoulders behind your hands
Rest your forearms on the person's chest
Bend your elbows to cushion each push
Keep them straight with your shoulders directly above your hands

questions.question: If a second trained rescuer is available, roughly how often should you swap over during CPR?
options: Only when the AED tells you to

questions.question: The recommended compression depth of 5 to 6 cm is described as roughly what fraction of the depth of the chest?
Where should one of the AED pads be placed?
options: On the right side below the armpit
On the stomach near the navel

questions.question: When calling 999 during a cardiac arrest, you should put your phone on speaker so your hands are free and the call handler can guide you.
If you are on your own and there is no AED with you, you should leave the person to go and find one.
An automated external defibrillator (AED) is designed so that untrained members of the public can use it.
For a child, you must always wait for paediatric pads and never use a standard adult AED.
Which of the following are dangers you should look for before approaching a collapsed person? (Select all that apply)
Which of the following should you tell the ambulance crew when you hand over? (Select all that apply)
options: Whether there is a DNACPR or ReSPECT form

questions.question: Which of the following help you stay safe when using an AED? (Select all that apply)
options: Dry the chest before attaching the pads
Keep the pads clear of medication patches
Keep touching the person while it analyses the rhythm
Move any oxygen mask away before a shock is given

questions.question: Why is the recovery position used for an unresponsive person who is breathing normally? (Select all that apply)
options: It lets any fluids or vomit drain out
It delivers a controlled electric shock

questions.question: Chest compressions on an adult should be at least 5 cm deep but no more than ___ cm.
The recovery position keeps the airway open and lets any fluids or vomit ___ out rather than blocking breathing.
When treating a conscious choking adult, if the cough is ineffective you give up to five back blows followed by up to five abdominal ___.
